meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering Client Workflows to Debug Code
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn an intermediate client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, build confidence, and scale your SaaS.
content.answer_block: To solve debugging gridlock, implement a structured client-side observability workflow. By integrating Antigravity for automated state snapshots and Claude Code for real-time iterative resolution, you transform opaque errors into actionable logs. This systematic approach isolates failures, accelerates root cause analysis, and turns every bug into a high-value learning milestone.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve been staring at a 'TypeError: Cannot read property of undefined' for three hours, and the silence of your empty IDE is deafening. You feel like an imposter, wondering if you’re cut out for this. We’ve all been there—staring at a wall of red text, feeling the weight of a project that refuses to compile.

But here is the secret: professional engineering isn't about knowing the answer; it’s about having a workflow that forces the answer to reveal itself. By shifting from 'guessing and checking' to a structured, AI-assisted diagnostic pipeline, you stop fighting the code and start mastering it. Let’s turn your debugging frustration into your most potent competitive advantage.

steps.title: Establish an Observability Baseline
steps.description: Before fixing, you must see the failure state clearly. Use Antigravity to inject automated state logging into your component lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this React component and inject high-fidelity state logging at every lifecycle transition. Ensure state changes are captured in a format readable by Claude Code.

steps.title: Capture the Failure Context
steps.description: When the bug occurs, you need a snapshot of the environment. Antigravity can freeze the execution context to prevent the bug from disappearing on refresh.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a diagnostic wrapper that captures the Redux store and local state whenever a console error is triggered.

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Feed your captured logs into Claude Code. By providing the specific error and the snapshot, you eliminate guesswork.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare the current failed state against the expected state defined in my unit tests. Identify the exact line of logic causing the mismatch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze the provided log file in /logs/error.json and suggest a fix for the logic flaw.'

steps.title: Iterative Patching via CLI
steps.description: Don't manually rewrite code. Use Claude Code to apply the fix and verify it immediately against your test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a patch for the identified logic error and ensure it adheres to the existing project coding style.

steps.title: Validation & Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure the fix didn't break other features. Use your AI workflow to run a full suite of regression tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the full test suite and verify that the specific bug fix has not introduced side effects in related components.

pro_tips: Never debug in production; always reproduce the bug in a local environment using a mock state generator.
Treat every error message as a 'missing requirement' rather than a failure of your ability.
Use Claude Code to document the 'Why' behind every fix in your commit messages for future reference.
Automate your logs to trigger a notification when a specific edge case occurs, allowing you to catch bugs before users do.
Master the art of 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude—explain the code line-by-line to the AI to uncover logic gaps yourself.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully debugged your first module, document the process in a README file. This creates a 'Debug Journal'—a powerful tool for your portfolio that proves to recruiters you can handle complex technical debt.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs hold your career back. Master the tools the pros use and start building production-ready apps today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
